title: santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. laxa
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. laxa, also known as White Lavender or Cotton Lavender, is a variety of the Santolina chamaecyparissus plant, a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. laxa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, while also serving as a natural remedy for anxiety and stress relief. Herbal preparations of Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. laxa include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to make salves, creams, and ointments for topical application.
